description:
You are acting as a 2nd-line Linux support engineer who has been asked to gather basic package-related diagnostics from a customer host so that another team can reproduce the environment in a lab.

1. Create a new directory  
   /home/user/support  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Inside that directory create a plain-text file named  
   /home/user/support/pkg_diag.log

3. Write exactly **three** lines to /home/user/support/pkg_diag.log (terminate each line with a single UNIX newline, including the last one) using the precise format shown below. No additional spaces, columns, or commentary are allowed.

   Line-1 format  
   TIMESTAMP=<ISO-8601-UTC>  
   • Replace <ISO-8601-UTC> with the current UTC time when you run the command, expressed in extended ISO-8601 combined date-and-time form and ending with the literal “Z”.  
   • Example of the required structure (your numbers will differ):  
     TIMESTAMP=2024-02-28T16:34:51Z  

   Line-2 format  
   PACKAGE=bash VERSION=<version_string> ARCH=<architecture>  
   • Extract the installed version **and** architecture of the package “bash” from the system’s package database (for APT systems this is normally “dpkg”).  
   • Example structure (version and architecture will differ):  
     PACKAGE=bash VERSION=5.1-6ubuntu7.2 ARCH=amd64  

   Line-3 format  
   TOTAL_PACKAGES=<integer>  
   • Replace <integer> with the exact count of packages that the package manager reports as installed at the moment you collect the data (just the number, no commas).  
   • Example structure:  
     TOTAL_PACKAGES=1654  

4. Verify that /home/user/support/pkg_diag.log has exactly three lines, follows the formats above **without extra whitespace**, and is world-readable (octal permissions 0644).

The automated grading system will open /home/user/support/pkg_diag.log and validate:

• The file exists.  
• It contains precisely three newline-terminated lines.  
• Line-1 matches the ISO-8601 layout with a trailing “Z”.  
• Line-2 correctly reports the installed bash version and architecture.  
• Line-3 is a positive integer equal to the system’s installed-package count.

No other output or files are required.
truth:
Expected artefacts and validation patterns (regular expressions):

1. Directory that must exist  
   /home/user/support  (permissions may be 0755 or stricter)

2. File that must exist  
   /home/user/support/pkg_diag.log  (permissions must be 0644 or stricter)

3. pkg_diag.log content rules (each “^ … $” is a full-line regex that the grader will apply):

   Line 1: ^TIMESTAMP=[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

   Line 2: ^PACKAGE=bash VERSION=[^ ]+ ARCH=[^ ]+$

   Line 3: ^TOTAL_PACKAGES=[0-9]+$

4. The file must contain exactly three lines in this order and must end with a newline character.

5. No additional files should be placed inside /home/user/support.
